title: sideritis arborescens subsp. ortonedae
intro: Sideritis arborescens subsp. ortonedae, also known as white mountain tea or Ortuneña, is a subspecies of the Sideritis arborescens plant, a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of white mountain tea include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health issues. White mountain tea can be prepared as a herbal tea by infusing the leaves in hot water, and it is also used in various herbal preparations, such as capsules and tinctures.
